summary: Comprehensive Docker security audit completed. Analyzed 28 Docker configuration files across Dockerfiles, docker-compose, and Kubernetes manifests. Found 1 critical issue (docker socket mount with root user), 3 high-risk issues (hardcoded credentials, weak secrets), and 4 medium-risk issues (missing USER directives, DEBUG mode, health checks).

critical_findings.title: Docker Socket Mount Without Privilege Boundary
critical_findings.description: aldeci-ui service mounts /var/run/docker.sock with user: root:root, granting complete Docker daemon access and host compromise capability
critical_findings.mitigation: Use Docker API via TCP+TLS, or run as non-root with Docker group, or remove mount if not essential

high_risk_findings.title: Hardcoded Database Password
high_risk_findings.risk: Plaintext PostgreSQL credential in version control, visible in logs and docker-compose output

high_risk_findings.title: Weak Default Kubernetes Secret
high_risk_findings.risk: Human-readable default secret would make all JWT tokens forgeable if deployed unchanged

high_risk_findings.title: Database URL with Embedded Credentials
high_risk_findings.risk: Credentials visible in container inspect; sslmode=disable means plaintext database connection

medium_risk_findings.title: Missing USER Directives (Root Execution)
medium_risk_findings.risk: Python containers run as root; RCE vulnerability + docker socket = host compromise

medium_risk_findings.title: DEBUG Mode Enabled in Integration Tests
medium_risk_findings.risk: DEBUG=1 may leak sensitive information in logs; verbose LocalStack output

medium_risk_findings.title: Missing Health Checks
medium_risk_findings.risk: Docker Compose cannot detect service failures; orchestration issues

medium_risk_findings.title: Hardcoded Demo Tokens in Dockerfiles
medium_risk_findings.risk: Tokens embedded in image layers; if images used in production without rebuild, security is compromised

issues: Docker socket mounted to aldeci-ui with root user access — critical host compromise risk
Kubernetes deployment secret = 'CHANGE_ME' — will be forgeable if not customized
5 Python Dockerfiles run as root — violates CIS Docker Benchmark 4.1
9 services missing health checks — reduces resilience and auto-recovery
3 demo Dockerfiles have hardcoded tokens — acceptable for demo but risk if reused
Database connection uses sslmode=disable — plaintext over network
LocalStack credentials visible in docker-compose output — acceptable for test, but pattern risk
No --cap-drop=ALL or security_opt in any compose file
DEBUG=1 in integration tests may leak sensitive logs

immediate_critical: Remove /var/run/docker.sock mount from docker-compose.aldeci-complete.yml or run as non-root
Enforce POSTGRES_PASSWORD via environment variable (use :? syntax)
Add Helm pre-install hook to reject FIXOPS_JWT_SECRET=CHANGE_ME
high_priority: Add USER directives to all 5 Python production Dockerfiles
Change DATABASE_URL sslmode to 'require' for TLS encryption
Add health checks to 9 missing services
Document in README that demo Dockerfiles should not go to production registry
medium_priority: Disable DEBUG=1 by default in docker-compose.integration.yml
Use Docker secrets for sensitive config instead of env vars
Add apt-get clean to all apt-get RUN commands
Exclude test/ and docs/ from COPY to reduce image size
low_priority: Update .dockerignore to exclude *.db and data/ directories
Consider Alpine variant for Python images (python:3.11-alpine)
Document port mapping rationale (currently all reasonable)
Add LABEL metadata to all Dockerfiles for traceability

senior_review_reason: Critical docker socket security issue and multiple hardcoded credentials require architectural decision on how to resolve. Recommend security-analyst review.
audit_tool: grep + Docker security best practices checklist (CIS Docker Benchmark)
cis_violations: 4.1: Ensure a user for the container has been created (5 Dockerfiles)
5.4: Ensure privileged containers are not used (docker socket + root)
5.3: Ensure Linux kernel capabilities are restricted (no --cap-drop)
5.25: Ensure container is restricted from acquiring additional privileges
